Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

There are references left in lustre/utils/llog_reader.c and lustre/doc/llapi_hsm_copytool_register.3 (this is what the '-1' review is about).

(just for the record) Before this patch, the coordinator never grouped requests of different types (archive/restore). This might have introduced a bias in the implementation of copytools that if the first request in an hal is of certain type, the others are of that same type. I checked for lhsmtool_posix and robinhood's "generic copytool", those are not impacted.

Patch Set 3:

> There are references left in lustre/utils/llog_reader.c

Quentin, I think we should just have llog_reader tell the truth.

Patch Set 3:

> I think we should just have llog_reader tell the truth

Can you explain the rationale behind this?

The documentation should still be updated, shouldn't it?

Patch Set 3:

>> I think we should just have llog_reader tell the truth

> Can you explain the rationale behind this?

Well it's a tool for experts who would probably prefer that it just shows whatever the llog record says rather than hiding deprecated values.

> The documentation should still be updated, shouldn't it?

Yes, I will update the manpage.

parents.subject: LU-10383 hsm: add action count to hsm scan data

commit.subject: LU-10383 hsm: ignore compound_id
commit.message:
LU-10383 hsm: ignore compound_id

Ignore request compound ids in the HSM coordinator. Compound ids
prevent batching of CDT to CT requests and degrade HSM
performance. Use CT/archive id compatabiliy when deciding which HSM
actions to put in a request.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-hsm
Signed-off-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Change-Id: I38513f3b75313eb78bfb9811ab4e40e3e2b904c7
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/30949
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Quentin Bouget <quentin.bouget@cea.fr>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Faccini Bruno <bruno.faccini@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
